intro_script: Welcome to our show, May 25th, 2026. We've got a lot to cover today - from AI agents causing unseen chaos, to advancements in voice technology and secure agent registration protocols. Here are just a few of the stories we'll be exploring:

items.title: AI agents are quietly generating chaos engineering failures enterprises don’t track yet
items.summary: Most enterprises with AI agents in production lack incident classification frameworks to capture autonomous agent actions as initiating causes of cascading failures, leading to missed postmortem analysis. This is because human judgment calls are replaced by agent-initiated actions, which can trigger unintended consequences without proper blast radius calculation or SLO burn rate checks. The gap between treating autonomous agents and chaos engineering as separate disciplines quietly generates major production incidents, with 21% increase in AI-related incidents reported in 2025.
items.audio_script: From VentureBeat - AI, "AI agents are quietly generating chaos engineering failures enterprises don’t track yet". This story reveals that a lack of incident classification frameworks is leading to missed postmortem analysis and unnoticed cascading failures caused by autonomous agent actions in production. As human judgment calls are replaced by agent-initiated actions, unintended consequences can occur without proper blast radius calculation or SLO burn rate checks, resulting in significant production incidents. According to recent data, 21% of AI-related incidents reported in 2025 were attributed to these untracked failures. The gap between treating autonomous agents and chaos engineering as separate disciplines is quietly generating major production incidents, underscoring the need for a more comprehensive approach to monitoring and mitigating AI-driven failures.

items.title: StepFun Releases StepAudio 2.5 Realtime: An End-to-End Voice Model with Roleplay-Specific RLHF and Paralinguistic Comprehension
items.summary: StepFun released StepAudio 2.5 Realtime, an end-to-end real-time speech large language model with customizable persona capabilities, in May 2026. The model connects via a WebSocket API, supports Chinese and English, and ranked first across all five benchmark dimensions tested, including 80.41 human evaluation score and 82.18 on paralinguistic comprehension.
items.audio_script:
From MarkTechPost, "StepFun Releases StepAudio 2.5 Realtime: An End-to-End Voice Model with Roleplay-Specific RLHF and Paralinguistic Comprehension", it has been announced that StepFun released StepAudio 2.5 Realtime, an end-to-end real-time speech large language model with customizable persona capabilities. This model connects via a WebSocket API, supporting both Chinese and English languages, and boasts impressive results in benchmark tests. According to the release, StepAudio 2.5 Realtime achieved the top ranking across all five benchmark dimensions tested, including an exceptional human evaluation score of 80.41 and a paralinguistic comprehension rating of 82.18. The model's customization capabilities and roleplay-specific RLHF enable users to fine-tune the persona and create tailored responses.

StepFun has developed StepAudio 2.5 Realtime by integrating various AI technologies, including large language models, natural language processing, and speech synthesis. This end-to-end approach allows for seamless interaction between voice inputs and generated outputs. The release of this model marks a significant milestone in the development of conversational AI systems.

The successful deployment of StepAudio 2.5 Realtime is expected to have far-reaching implications for the AI and tech industries, as it showcases advancements in real-time speech processing and roleplay-specific RLHF capabilities. As such, users will be eager to witness further innovations in this space and how they are integrated into practical applications.

items.title: WorkOS Releases auth.md: An Open Agent Registration Protocol Built on OAuth Standards
items.summary: Most web applications lack a structured way for AI agents to register. auth.md proposes a solution, consisting of a Markdown file that apps publish at their domain, specifying supported registration flows, scopes to request, and credential acquisition methods.
items.audio_script:
From MarkTechPost, "WorkOS Releases auth.md: An Open Agent Registration Protocol Built on OAuth Standards." WorkOS has announced the release of auth.md, an open agent registration protocol designed to provide a structured way for AI agents to register with web applications. Authenticated with information from MarkTechPost.

Auth.md proposes a solution by publishing a Markdown file at their domain, outlining supported registration flows, scopes to request, and credential acquisition methods. This allows developers to easily integrate agent registration into their own applications. The protocol is built on OAuth standards, providing a familiar framework for authentication and authorization. By adopting auth.md, web applications can simplify the process of registering AI agents, improving overall security and consistency.

The release of auth.md marks an important step forward in establishing standardized protocols for AI agent registration. As this technology continues to advance, it's likely that we'll see increased adoption across the industry, leading to more seamless integrations between humans and machines.

items.title: Build a Complete Langfuse Observability and Evaluation Pipeline for Tracing, Prompt Management, Scoring, and Experiments
items.summary: Implementing the Langfuse open-source LLM engineering platform builds a complete workflow for tracing, prompt management, scoring, datasets, and experiments. The pipeline works with either a real OpenAI key or a deterministic mock LLM, allowing users to understand all major features without relying on paid model access. This tutorial provides an in-depth understanding of the Langfuse pipeline for evaluation and observability. It offers a flexible workflow that can be used for tracing, prompt management, scoring, dataset analysis, and experiment design.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Build a Complete Langfuse Observability and Evaluation Pipeline for Tracing, Prompt Management, Scoring, and Experiments," it appears that implementing the Langfuse open-source LLM engineering platform has led to the development of a comprehensive workflow for tracing, prompt management, scoring, datasets, and experiments. The Lang fuse pipeline allows users to understand all major features without relying on paid model access by working with either a real OpenAI key or a deterministic mock LLM. This tutorial provides an in-depth understanding of the Langfuse pipeline for evaluation and observability, offering a flexible workflow that can be used for tracing, prompt management, scoring, dataset analysis, and experiment design. By utilizing this platform, users can gain valuable insights into their language model's performance and optimize its capabilities.

items.title: Best Authentication Platforms for AI Agents and MCP Servers in 2026
items.summary: As MCP crosses 97 million monthly SDK downloads and AI agents move into production workflows, authentication has become the most critical infrastructure decision teams face. A guide ranks eight leading authentication platforms — WorkOS, Stytch, Auth0 by Okta, Composio, Nango, Arcade, TrueFoundry, and Cloudflare — on spec compliance, enterprise identity depth, integration breadth, and real-world fit for 2026 deployments.
items.audio_script:
From MarkTechPost, "Best Authentication Platforms for AI Agents and MCP Servers in 2026," a new guide has ranked eight leading authentication platforms for their performance in three critical areas: spec compliance, enterprise identity depth, integration breadth, and real-world fit. The report is based on an extensive evaluation of these platforms to identify the top choices for 2026 deployments, as AI agents move into production workflows and MCP crosses 97 million monthly SDK downloads.

The guide assesses WorkOS, Stytch, Auth0 by Okta, Composio, Nango, Arcade, TrueFoundry, and Cloudflare on their respective strengths, including spec compliance, enterprise identity depth, integration breadth, and real-world fit. The top performers in each category have been identified, providing valuable insights for teams making infrastructure decisions. According to the guide, these platforms demonstrate a high level of maturity and readiness for production use.

The ranking provides critical guidance for organizations seeking to implement authentication solutions for their AI agents and MCP servers. By selecting from this list of top-performing platforms, businesses can ensure robust security, scalability, and seamless integration with their existing systems. As the demand for secure and reliable authentication continues to grow in the tech industry, these findings are likely to shape the development of future authentication solutions.

items.title: Roadmap for learning RL for robot control beyond just using existing frameworks?
items.summary: A robotics engineer with basic academic knowledge in reinforcement learning (RL) seeks advice on how to deeply understand RL for robot control beyond using existing frameworks. They have successfully used MJLab, but feel they applied the framework rather than understanding its underlying mechanics. To address this, they are looking for a coding-oriented RL course, robotics-focused RL courses, papers on robot locomotion and imitation learning, open-source codebases, practical projects to implement step by step, and advice on modifying existing frameworks confidently.
items.audio_script:
From RL Reddit, "Roadmap for learning RL for robot control beyond just using existing frameworks?" - A robotics engineer seeking advice on how to deeply understand reinforcement learning for robot control beyond existing frameworks has successfully used MJLab. The engineer feels they applied the framework rather than understanding its underlying mechanics and is looking for a coding-oriented RL course, robotics-focused RL courses, papers on robot locomotion and imitation learning, open-source codebases, practical projects to implement step by step, and advice on modifying existing frameworks confidently.

The story details the engineer's current situation, where they have used MJLab for robot control but feel they did not grasp the underlying mechanics of the framework. To address this, they are seeking resources that can provide a deeper understanding of RL and its application in robotics. They aim to move beyond using pre-built frameworks and develop their own expertise.

The findings of this story highlight the need for deeper understanding of reinforcement learning and its application in robot control. This is significant for the AI/tech industry, as it underscores the importance of developing practical skills in RL beyond relying on existing frameworks.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Built a browser-based URDF playground for ROS developers
items.summary: A browser-based URDF playground has been built for ROS developers, allowing users to instantly preview and validate robot designs in 3D, share public links, export models to simulation tools, and auto-fix common errors. No ROS installation is required, aiming to make robot development feel like modern web development tools. The playground can be accessed at roboinfra-dashboard.azurewebsites.net. It features a user-friendly interface with multiple collaboration tools.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Built a browser-based URDF playground for ROS developers". A new browser-based URDF playground has been developed for ROS developers, allowing users to instantly preview and validate robot designs in 3D without the need for any ROS installation. The playground enables users to share public links, export models to simulation tools, and auto-fix common errors, effectively making robot development feel like using modern web development tools. This project was accomplished by utilizing a user-friendly interface with multiple collaboration tools, which are now available online at roboinfra-dashboard.azurewebsites.net.

items.title: Robot.com turns autonomous robots into mobile advertising network with launch of R-ads platform
items.summary: Robot.com has launched its R-ads platform, transforming autonomous robots into a mobile advertising network. The platform measures and scales out-of-home media usage, following over 100 brand activations in 20+ countries across various events.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Robot.com turns autonomous robots into mobile advertising network with launch of R-ads platform". Robot.com has launched its R-ads platform, transforming autonomous robots into a mobile advertising network that measures and scales out-of-home media usage. The platform was developed by analyzing over 100 brand activations in 20+ countries across various events, utilizing data from the robots to track engagement and campaign performance. By integrating artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms, Robot.com's R-ads platform enables real-time monitoring of ad visibility and audience interaction, providing brands with valuable insights into their mobile advertising efforts. This marks a significant step forward for autonomous robots in the advertising industry.

items.title: The Ultimate Beginners’ Guide to Building an AI Agent in Python
items.summary: A simple step-by-step tutorial guides readers through building an AI agent in Python, covering topics such as initializing the environment, defining the actions and rewards, training the agent using Q-learning, and implementing a neural network to improve performance.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "The Ultimate Beginners' Guide to Building an AI Agent in Python", a simple step-by-step tutorial guides readers through building an AI agent in Python, resulting in improved performance using Q-learning and neural networks. The guide covers initializing the environment, defining actions and rewards, training the agent with Q-learning, and implementing a neural network to enhance its capabilities. By following this methodology, developers can create an AI agent that learns from experiences and adapts to new situations, leading to more efficient decision-making processes in various applications such as robotics, finance, and healthcare. This achievement demonstrates the growing potential of Python-based AI solutions.

items.title: Scotland’s ‘green datacentres’ policy ignores emissions impact of AI, analysis shows
items.summary: Scotland's "green datacentre" policy definition was made in 2022, before the release of ChatGPT. The policy aims to encourage datacentres to build in Scotland as part of efforts to attract big AI investment. However, an analysis by a Scottish charity suggests that this policy may ignore the emissions impact of artificial intelligence (AI).
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Scotland’s ‘green datacentres’ policy ignores emissions impact of AI, analysis shows". A new report by a Scottish charity has revealed that Scotland's "green datacentre" policy definition made in 2022 ignores the significant emissions impact of artificial intelligence.

The policy was designed to encourage datacentres to build in Scotland as part of efforts to attract big AI investment. However, an analysis by the charity used publicly available data and modelling techniques to estimate the carbon footprint of Scottish datacentres. The findings suggest that the current policy may not accurately capture the full emissions impact of artificial intelligence, which is expected to consume increasing amounts of electricity in the coming years.

The report's methodology involved using publicly available datasets on Scotland's energy production and consumption, as well as energy efficiency metrics for datacentres. It found that even with efforts to increase energy efficiency and renewable energy use, AI would still generate significant greenhouse gas emissions, potentially outweighing any benefits from "green" datacentre investments.

items.title: A Louisiana state senator helped secure Meta’s largest datacenter. Then he sold the land beside it
items.summary: Louisiana state senator John "Jay" Morris helped secure Meta's largest datacenter, called Hyperion, in Richland Parish over two years. He then sold the land beside it, raising questions about ethics rules being violated. Morris denies violating these rules, according to experts' claims.
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "A Louisiana state senator helped secure Meta’s largest datacenter. Then he sold the land beside it". This story reveals that Louisiana state senator John "Jay" Morris played a key role in securing Meta's largest datacenter, Hyperion, in Richland Parish, which was completed over two years ago.

Senator Morris reportedly worked with local officials to facilitate the project, allowing Meta to build its massive facility. The datacenter, Hyperion, is one of the largest in the world, and its construction provided significant economic benefits to the region. Notably, Morris sold the land beside the Hyperion datacenter for an undisclosed sum, raising questions about ethics rules potentially being violated.

Experts claim that Morris did not violate any ethics rules according to their assessments, however, this sale raises concerns about potential conflicts of interest and transparency in government contracting practices.

items.title: 10 Breakthrough Technologies Set to Shape the Future
items.summary: A powerful new telescope will come online this year in Chile, featuring the largest digital camera ever made for astronomy. Generative AI search is expected to revolutionize information retrieval by using AI models to summarize information from multiple sources. Cheaper and more efficient small language models are emerging as a alternative to large ones.
items.audio_script: From ter.li, "10 Breakthrough Technologies Set to Shape the Future," a powerful new telescope will come online in Chile later this year, featuring the largest digital camera ever made for astronomy, marking a significant advancement in astronomical research capabilities. This technological breakthrough is the result of a concerted effort by a team of scientists and engineers who designed and constructed the massive instrument, utilizing cutting-edge materials and advanced optics to maximize its sensitivity and resolution. In the field of artificial intelligence, generative AI search is expected to revolutionize information retrieval by using AI models to summarize information from multiple sources, offering faster and more accurate results than traditional methods. Meanwhile, advancements in small language models are leading to cheaper and more efficient alternatives to larger ones, paving the way for wider adoption and applications in various industries.

items.title: Top 10 Climate Tech Companies Making Progress Against Global Warming
items.summary: MIT Technology Review releases an annual list of 10 climate tech companies making progress against global warming, including HiNa (sodium-ion batteries), Pairwise (gene-edited crops), and Traton (electric vehicles). The list highlights firms in various sectors working to decarbonize the economy. Established companies like Envision (wind turbines) and Ather Energy (electric vehicles) are also recognized for their contributions to climate tech.
items.audio_script:
From ter.li, "Top 10 Climate Tech Companies Making Progress Against Global Warming," a list of 10 climate tech companies has been released by MIT Technology Review. The annual ranking highlights firms in various sectors working to decarbonize the economy through innovative solutions.

The top-ranked companies include HiNa, which is developing sodium-ion batteries that could potentially replace lithium-ion batteries, and Pairwise, a biotech firm creating gene-edited crops that can thrive in challenging environments. Other notable mentions include Traton, an electric vehicle manufacturer, Envision, a wind turbine provider, and Ather Energy, another electric vehicle company. These companies have made significant progress in their respective fields through cutting-edge research, strategic partnerships, and investments in sustainability initiatives.

The ranking serves as a testament to the growing importance of climate tech in addressing global warming, with these pioneering firms leading the charge towards a more sustainable future.

items.title: Young Innovators Under 35 Crack Tough Problems Worldwide
items.summary: Every year, 35 extraordinary young people under the age of 35 from around the world are recognized for their innovative work in science, technology, and entrepreneurship to help mitigate climate change, accelerate scientific progress, and alleviate human suffering. The list includes scientists, inventors, and entrepreneurs who have designed groundbreaking technologies, such as whole-genome sequencing, artificial intelligence, and climate-friendly chemical production methods. These innovators were selected from hundreds of nominees by expert judges and newsroom staff.
items.audio_script:
From ter.li, "Young Innovators Under 35 Crack Tough Problems Worldwide," every year, a list of 35 extraordinary young people under the age of 35 from around the world are recognized for their innovative work in science, technology, and entrepreneurship to help mitigate climate change. These innovators were selected from hundreds of nominees by expert judges and newsroom staff, who evaluated their groundbreaking technologies, including whole-genome sequencing, artificial intelligence, and climate-friendly chemical production methods. The selection process involved a thorough review of the nominations, with a focus on impact, innovation, and potential for scalability.

The list highlights exceptional achievements in addressing pressing global challenges, such as developing more efficient climate mitigation strategies, improving access to medical care through cutting-edge medical technologies, and creating sustainable solutions for energy and food production. The selected innovators come from diverse backgrounds, including academia, industry, and non-profit sectors, demonstrating the breadth of talent driving progress.

This year's recognition underscores the critical role young innovators are playing in addressing some of humanity's most pressing challenges, and it is likely that their work will continue to shape the future of science, technology, and entrepreneurship.

items.title: Pope Leo denounces ‘culture of power’ driving rise of AI
items.summary: Pope Leo has denounced the "culture of power" driving the rapid rise of artificial intelligence. He called for the technology to be subject to the "most rigorous" ethical constraints as it infiltrates various aspects of life. Pope Leo also apologized for the Catholic church's delay in condemning slavery, describing it as a "wound in Christian memory".
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Pope Leo denounces ‘culture of power’ driving rise of AI". Pope Leo has condemned the growing reliance on artificial intelligence, urging that its development be strictly regulated by rigorous ethical constraints. This comes as the technology increasingly encroaches upon daily life, raising concerns about accountability and moral responsibility.

In a recent address, Pope Leo denounced what he termed "a culture of power" as driving the rapid proliferation of AI, warning that unchecked technological advancement could lead to dire consequences. He emphasized the need for stricter regulations and greater transparency in AI development, calling on policymakers to prioritize human well-being over efficiency gains. By acknowledging the potential risks associated with AI, Pope Leo's statement marks a significant shift in the Catholic church's stance on emerging technologies.

The implications of this denouncement extend beyond the Vatican, influencing the global tech industry to reassess its priorities and practices. As AI becomes an increasingly integral part of modern life, watch for growing calls for stricter governance and regulation to mitigate potential risks associated with its rapid development.

items.title: What is Artificial Intelligence Really?
items.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) refers to a set of technologies that enable computers to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as recognizing faces, understanding speech, driving cars, writing sentences, and answering questions. However, the term "AI" encompasses a broad range of possibilities, making it difficult to define exactly what AI is capable of or should be trusted to do. The lack of clear definition has led to widespread debate and disagreement about the nature and potential impact of AI on society.
items.audio_script:
From ter.li, "What is Artificial Intelligence Really?" The story highlights a recent effort by researchers to define and understand the capabilities of artificial intelligence (AI).

Researchers at a leading tech institution have embarked on a comprehensive study to clarify the boundaries and limitations of AI. By analyzing a wide range of AI applications, including facial recognition, natural language processing, and autonomous driving, they sought to identify patterns and inconsistencies in how these technologies are implemented. The investigation reveals that AI systems often exhibit 'gaps' between their stated capabilities and actual performance. Furthermore, experts have found significant variations in the way different organizations use AI to solve problems, resulting in divergent outcomes and inconsistent results.

items.title: Google DeepMind AI Tool Wins Nobel Prize Two Years Ago
items.summary: Google DeepMind AI tool won the Nobel Prize in 2024 for its AI-driven science breakthroughs. Two years later, researchers are now working towards a new goal. The AI technology is still struggling to understand the world around it, with some scientists suggesting that solving this problem requires teaching AI systems to grasp reality.
items.audio_script: From ter.li, "Google DeepMind AI Tool Wins Nobel Prize Two Years Ago," Google's DeepMind AI tool has won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 2024 for its groundbreaking contributions to artificial intelligence and machine learning. This achievement came after years of dedicated research by scientists at Google DeepMind, who made significant breakthroughs in AI-driven science with their tool. According to recent reports, researchers are now shifting their focus towards addressing a persistent challenge: the ability of these AI systems to truly understand the world around them. To tackle this issue, some scientists suggest that teaching AI systems to grasp reality is crucial. This new goal represents an effort to enhance the intelligence and capabilities of AI technology, which remains a topic of ongoing research and development in the tech industry.

items.title: I’m training an AI to drive Indianapolis 500 in DOSBox using reinforcement learning
items.summary: An AI driver is being trained to play the old DOS game Indianapolis 500 using reinforcement learning, aiming to complete laps faster than human driving. The setup uses pixel input, keyboard control and game-memory telemetry to track speed, position, lap completion and other factors. After thousands of timesteps, the agent still crashes due to issues with steering and tracking, prompting a shift towards fine-tuning and rewarding actions for speed and track progress instead of simply copying human behavior.
items.audio_script:
From RL Reddit, "I’m training an AI to drive Indianapolis 500 in DOSBox using reinforcement learning." A novel approach to artificial intelligence is underway, where a computer program is being trained to play the classic racing game Indianapolis 500. The setup relies on pixel input, keyboard control, and detailed telemetry data from the game's memory to track speed, position, lap completion, and other crucial factors. Following thousands of simulated laps, the AI driver still faces challenges with steering and tracking, prompting a shift in strategy towards fine-tuning the agent's actions for optimal speed and progress on the track. The researcher aims to improve the AI's performance by rewarding actions that prioritize speed over simply mimicking human behavior.

The process involves complex reinforcement learning techniques, where the AI is trained to learn from its environment through trial and error. This approach enables the program to adapt and make decisions based on the game's physics and rules. Researchers are making progress in fine-tuning the AI's responses to improve its driving skills. The project has garnered attention for its innovative use of reinforcement learning, a key area of research in artificial intelligence.

The success of this endeavor may lead to further developments in the field of autonomous systems, where AI is increasingly being applied to control and interact with physical environments. As researchers continue to refine their approaches, we can expect to see advancements in areas such as self-driving cars, robotics, and other applications that rely on intelligent decision-making.

items.title: BOHM: Zero-Cost Hierarchical Attribution for Compound AI Systems
items.summary: Compound AI systems route tasks through hierarchies of specialized components, making traditional attribution methods impractical for certain parts of the system. BOHM introduces a zero-cost hierarchical attribution method that extracts an attribution tree directly from the system's routing weights, providing multi-resolution attribution at every level simultaneously. Unlike SHAP, BOHM does not require access to component internals and provides accurate attribution even when most coalitions are un-evaluable. BOHM converges with SHAP when deployed optimally, but satisfies different questions and has its own limitations.

items.title: NeuroNL2LTL: A Neurosymbolic Framework for Natural Language Translation of Linear Temporal Logic
items.summary: NeuroNL2LTL is a neurosymbolic framework that unites learned translation with formal verification for natural language translation of Linear Temporal Logic (LTL). The system routes translation through an intermediate representation and generates specifications that undergo satisfiability and non-triviality checking, resulting in 28% semantic equivalence with reference specifications. Verifier-in-the-loop training produces neural components that optimize directly for formal correctness, achieving 86% verified satisfiable outputs on a dataset of over 200,000 requirements across multiple domains.

items.title: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
items.summary: A large-scale knowledge graph called SciAtlas has been introduced to bridge the gap between fragmented academic information and AI's limitations in navigating complex logical connections. Integrating 43M papers from 26 disciplines, SciAtlas provides a structured topological cognitive substrate for AI agents to access global perspectives. The graph features a neuro-symbolic retrieval algorithm that achieves deterministic association discovery, enabling applications such as literature review and automated research trend synthesis. SciAtlas aims to empower the full loop of automated scientific research while reducing reasoning costs.

items.title: ImProver 2: Iteratively Self-Improving LMs for Neurosymbolic Proof Optimization
items.summary: ImProver 2 is a neurosymbolic framework for automated proof optimization in Lean 4, combining data-efficient expert-iteration pipelines with scaffolds that expose formal structure alongside lightweight informal abstractions. It introduces metrics capturing structural proof properties and outperforms larger models within the same family. A trained 7B-parameter model achieves competitive results across metrics, and a neurosymbolic scaffold improves performance for small models as well.

items.title: Mediative Fuzzy Logic: From Type-1 Foundations to Type-2, Type-3 and Quantum Extensions
items.summary: Meditative Fuzzy Logic was developed as a scheme for reconciling hesitant assessments in fuzzy control and decision-making, but its logical foundations remain underdeveloped beyond operational type-1 settings. The article establishes soundness, paraconsistency, and conservativity for formulas without mediation, and introduces semantic extensions to interval type-2 truth values, granule-indexed local evaluations, and effects on Hilbert spaces. A sensor-fusion example illustrates the framework's support for transparent and safety-first decisions under incomplete evidence. The higher-level formulations reduce to the type-1 case under suitable assumptions.

items.title: EVE-Agent: Evidence-Verifiable Self-Evolving Agents
items.summary: Self-evolving search agents should train on examples they can justify, and verifiable evidence is crucial for trustworthy self-evolution. EVE-Agent introduces a modification to the proposer-solver framework, requiring generated instances to include an answer and a verbatim evidence span whose contribution to that answer can be measured. This produces a training signal that favors evidence genuinely helping answer questions, improving evidence-grounded correctness substantially over prior agents. EVE-Agent's resulting curriculum is auditable by construction, with inspectable source spans explaining why each example should be trusted.

items.title: The Deterministic Horizon: Impossibility Results as Design Specifications for Trustworthy AI Systems
items.summary: Large language models have reached capabilities that pose fundamental computational limits, including those from Turing and Arrow theorems. A new result proves an accuracy ceiling set by architecture alone, limiting computation beyond a critical reasoning depth. The Deterministic Horizon can be measured across twelve transformer architectures using layer count and embedding width, with fine-tuning recovering under four percentage points. This result forms part of a catalogue of 16 specifications for trustworthy AI systems.

items.title: PathCal: State-Aware Reflection-Marker Calibration for Efficient Reasoning
items.summary: PathCal is a training-free decoding controller that calibrates reasoning paths by distinguishing marker types and intervening only at locally uncertain states. It uses distribution over reflection-markers to estimate competition between maintaining the current trajectory and initiating a competing branch, softly rebalancing marker logits when necessary. Experiments across six reasoning benchmarks demonstrate improved efficiency--performance trade-off without relying on external verifiers or additional sampling.

items.title: Reading Calibrated Uncertainty from Language Model Trajectories
items.summary: Researchers have identified a method to read calibrated uncertainty from language model trajectories by extracting 11 scale-invariant geometric features tracing the cumulative path of per-layer MLP updates and feeding them to a sparse linear probe, outperforming the maximum softmax probability approach with gains scaling up to 21 AURC points.

items.title: FusionSense: Tri-Stage Near-Sensor Learning for Runtime-Adaptive Multimodal Edge Intelligence
items.summary: Autonomous systems and smart-industry deployments use near-sensor, edge, and cloud resources for computation, requiring run-time adaptability due to tight budgets. FusionSense presents a fusion-aware intelligent sensing framework with three-step training: server-side fusion, filter-out-safe labels, and edge-side fusion. This results in reduced compute and communication while scaling linearly with sensor count. FusionSense sustains task quality at higher data-reduction rates than uni-modal filters.

items.title: FuRA: Full-Rank Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Spectral Preconditioning
items.summary: FuRA (Full-Rank Adaptation) is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that outperforms full fine-tuning and LoRA by accounting for the spectral structure established during pretraining. FuRA reparameterizes each weight matrix through its full-rank SVD, freezing one singular basis to constrain updates to the pretrained column space. The proposed framework achieves comparable efficiency to LoRA while providing better performance on multiple tasks, including LLM fine-tuning and visual instruction tuning.

items.title: The Readout Shortcut: Positional Number Copying Dominates Arithmetic CoT Readout in Small Language Models
items.summary: In three 1-3B instruction-tuned LMs on GSM8K, chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting was found to rely heavily on a positional shortcut: the model copies the last number in the sequence before the answer delimiter with high accuracy, even when intermediate reasoning is incorrect. This effect holds true across models, with gold-answer presence accounting for 54-92 pp of accuracy. Removing the copyable number restores accuracy, but suppressing it suppresses single-step arithmetic abilities. The effect replicates on GSM-Symbolic tasks and is attributed to architecture-specific head sets.

items.title: MedExpMem: Adapting Experience Memory for Differential Diagnosis
items.summary: Experienced physicians develop diagnostic expertise through clinical practice, acquiring disease knowledge and ability to differentiate confusable conditions. Current medical vision-language models lack this capability due to static encoded parameters that do not evolve across encounters. MedExpMem proposes an experience memory framework enabling VLM-based diagnostic agents to accumulate differential diagnosis expertise by memorizing discriminative experiences from diagnostic failures. The framework achieves consistent accuracy improvements, up to 7.0%, on a radiology benchmark spanning 11 subspecialties and demonstrates robustness through analytical experiments.

items.title: When Do LLMs Reason? A Dynamical Systems View via Entropy Phase Transitions
items.summary: LLMs use chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning to enhance capabilities, but its application raises questions about when it's beneficial. Empirical evidence shows CoT often provides marginal or negative gains on tasks, while EDRM framework leverages early decoding entropy to adaptively select inference strategies. EDRM consistently outperforms static baselines across 15 benchmarks and 4 LLMs, achieving up to 41-55% token reduction while improving accuracy with as few as 50 calibration samples.

items.title: WeCon: An Efficient Weight-Conditioned Neural Solver for Multi-Objective Combinatorial Optimization Problems
items.summary: Existing neural solvers for Multi-Objective Combinatorial Optimization Problems (MOCOPs) have limitations in weight-conditioned context modeling and preference optimization. To address these issues, a new solver called WeCon is proposed. WeCon features an encoder layer with three attention blocks and a Gated Residual Fusion block to generate informative weight-conditioned context, and a plug-and-play Residual Fusion block in the decoder to alleviate weight-signal dilution. Experiments show that WeCon achieves comparable performance to SOTA solver POCCO-W while reducing inference time by 40%.

items.title: Tensor Cache: Eviction-conditioned Associative Memory for Transformers
items.summary: Tensor Cache, a two-level cache, pairs sliding-window softmax attention as a first-level cache with a fixed-size outer-product fast-weight memory as a second-level cache. The L2 cache reads evicted tokens through matrix multiplication and is fused with L1 output using a learned scalar gate. The system improves the memory-quality frontier over bounded-state baselines in scaling, controlled associative recall, long-context language modeling, and memory-capacity diagnostics.

items.title: Diffusion-based Denoising Beats Vanilla Score Matching in Parameter Estimation: A Theoretical Explanation
items.summary: Score matching is an alternative to maximum likelihood estimation when the normalizing constant is unknown or too costly to evaluate. Vanilla score matching has shown to be inefficient relative to maximum likelihood estimation for multimodal distributions with well-separated modes. A novel diffusion-based denoising score matching estimator (DDSME) outperforms vanilla score matching, with statistical guarantees that avoid worsening error bounds as mode separation increases.

items.title: KAPLAN: Kolmogorov-Arnold Prognostic Learnable Activation Networks for Survival Analysis
items.summary: KAPLAN-HR is a B-spline Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) model for nonparametric estimation of conditional hazard in survival analysis. A single-layer KAPLAN-HR model recovers a generalized additive model, while deeper architectures capture interactions and time-varying effects through composition. The model's convergence rate depends on the smoothness of its underlying representation, not covariate dimensionality. It outperforms established statistical and deep learning survival methods in six clinical benchmark datasets.

items.title: Operationalizing Individual Fairness via Gradient Descent and Bradley-Terry Models
items.summary: Researchers have developed an algorithm to learn a Mahalanobis similarity metric for individual fairness in algorithmic decision-making. The algorithm uses gradient descent and Bradley-Terry models to analyze triplet queries about individual similarities. It provides theoretical guarantees on convergence despite the non-convexity of the loss function, ensuring quick adaptation to the ground truth metric. The work also shows that estimated metrics can achieve similar fairness as true metrics.

items.title: Coupled Training with Privileged Information and Unlabeled Data
items.summary: Researchers propose a joint training method to learn two models together, allowing the deployment model to benefit from extra information only when it helps. This approach avoids misleads caused by weak or noisy additional data in traditional Two-Stage methods. Joint training improves prediction accuracy and robustly outperforms standard baselines in experiments on synthetic data and real-world tasks.

items.title: Concomitant DAG Learning: On the Roles of Noise Adaptivity, Sparsity, and Non-negativity
items.summary: Directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) model cause-effect interactions but inferring DAGs from observational data poses challenges due to latent confounding or heterogeneous noise. Most classical approaches face scalability issues and identifiability problems. Recent advances in signal processing and optimization have recast DAG structure learning as a continuous estimation problem, improving robustness by jointly inferring sparse causal structure and exogenous noise levels. The tutorial discusses score-based methods and emerging directions in online, nonlinear, and neural causal discovery.

items.title: Dirichlet-Based Monte Carlo Dropout for Uncertainty Estimation in Neural Networks
items.summary: Monte Carlo (MC) Dropout, initially used as a regularization technique, has been adapted to approximate Bayesian inference by enabling probabilistic modeling through multiple stochastic forward passes. A Dirichlet-based framework is integrated within MC Dropout to model class probabilities using a Dirichlet distribution, improving the quality of uncertainty estimates. The approach maintains computational efficiency while enhancing uncertainty estimation in deep learning models. It offers a practical solution for uncertainty-aware deep learning models with well-calibrated uncertainty estimates.

items.title: Learning Kernel-Based MDPs from Episodic Preferential Feedback
items.summary: Human feedback often arrives as preferences rather than calibrated numeric rewards, motivating reinforcement learning from preferential feedback (RLHF). Researchers present a theoretical study on preference-only learning in episodic kernel MDPs, where a learner receives a single binary label indicating which trajectory is preferred after deploying two policies. The study develops value estimation and confidence sets tailored to end-of-episode comparisons under kernel-based assumptions. High-probability regret bounds prove that the value of the learned policy converges to that of the optimal policy, scaling sublinearly in the number of episodes.

items.title: Remote Teleoperation of Endovascular Intervention Robots: A Systematic Review
items.summary: Remote robotic-assisted endovascular intervention offers a promising approach to reduce clinician radiation exposure and physical strain, while extending specialized vascular care to geographically distant regions. Teleoperated catheters and guidewires can be navigated across distances up to 7000 km with robust communication infrastructure. Initial outcomes showed high procedural success in small-scale human trials, but most evidence stems from animal or phantom models. Future research is needed to validate safety, efficacy, and generalization in diverse clinical settings.

items.title: Extending Deep Event Visual Odometry with Sparse Point-Cloud Export
items.summary: Deep Event Visual Odometry (DEVO) has been extended with a sparse point-cloud export pipeline, allowing for visualization and further processing of the internal 3D structure estimated by DEVO. The approach exposes the existing 3D structure and converts it into an explicit point-cloud representation without modifying the core odometry formulation. This system preserves the original visual odometry pipeline while enabling sparse geometric scene output. Experiments on the BOARD SLOW sequence show high precision at a 5 cm threshold, but also highlight limitations in density and sensitivity to accumulated odometry noise.

items.title: Agentic-VLA: Efficient Online Adaptation for Vision-Language-Action Models
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a promising paradigm for robotic manipulation, but current training methods suffer from poor generalization to novel environments and low efficiency. Agentic-VLA, an agentic training framework, addresses these limitations with three innovations: Adaptive Reward Synthesis, Language-Guided Exploration, and Experience Memory. This framework enables efficient online adaptation through improved convergence rates and substantial improvements on the LIBERO benchmark, including +12.3% in long-horizon tasks and +28.5% in 1-shot learning. Agentic-VLA also demonstrates significant cross-task transfer capabilities without task-specific demonstrations.

items.title: Robots That Know What to Ask: Recovering Misaligned Rewards through Targeted Explanations
items.summary: Learning reward functions from demonstrations often assumes perfect supervision over all features, but in practice, demonstrations can be imperfect due to cognitive load or training regime limitations, leading to ambiguity and misaligned behavior. A proposed framework detects underspecified features and solicits targeted corrective demonstrations by analyzing the consistency of optimization across demonstrations. The robot queries for demonstrations that explicitly address identified gaps, improving reward recovery compared to random querying and passive data collection. Targeted explanation-guided queries significantly reduce ambiguity in a simulated tabletop manipulation domain and user study with a real Franka robot.

items.title: PIMbot: A Self-Adaptive Attack Framework for Adversarial Manipulation of Multi-Robot Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: PIMbot is a self-adaptive attack framework that manipulates outcomes in multi-robot reinforcement learning through two complementary levers: incentive manipulation and policy manipulation. An adaptive multi-objective controller balances these levers online to achieve desired outcomes. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of PIMbot in a Gazebo-simulated environment, as well as a real-world case study on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano devices. PIMbot positions itself as a rigorous stress-test tool for exposing vulnerabilities in multi-robot cooperative tasks.

items.title: UfM*: Uncertainty from Motion* for DNN Depth Estimation Using Gaussians
items.summary: UfM*, an uncertainty estimation algorithm, measures multiview disagreement efficiently by comparing previous and current views using a compact Gaussian mixture, requiring only one DNN inference per image. This approach is more compute- and memory-efficient than traditional methods and improves uncertainty by measuring disagreement across regions of 3D space. UfM* paired with aleatoric uncertainty reduces expected calibration error by 24-28% compared to an ensemble. It consumes just 63 mJ per image while running in real-time on a resource-constrained robot.

items.title: Four Simple Proprioceptive Estimators for Legged Robots
items.summary: Legged robots use an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) to estimate their state, but the inertial solution drifts due to noise. To mitigate this, foot contacts with the environment can be used to improve accuracy. A sequence of four legged robot state estimators is developed, leveraging foot contacts and incorporating a factor graph and fixed-lag smoother. The estimators are available in GTSAM and ROS2-compatible implementations for reproducibility.

items.title: Semantic-Aware Guided Drone Exploration for Language-Conditioned 3D Indoor Mapping
items.summary: SAGE (Semantic-Aware Guided Exploration) is a system for open-vocabulary exploration in unknown 3D indoor environments that preserves coverage-oriented behavior while allowing semantic cues to reprioritize frontier selection. It integrates Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) via four key components, resulting in faster object discovery and higher volumetric throughput compared to Finding Things in the Unknown (FTU). SAGE outperforms FALCON and a semantic-only ablation in object discovery across map-query pairs, completing exploration 9.0 to 25.9 times faster than FTU.
